domain: forest succession + shade-tolerance (4-stage stand dynamics theory: stand-initiation/stem-exclusion/understory-reinitiation/old-growth; pioneer-vs-climax species ecology; gap dynamics drive shade-tolerant recruitment)
specific_mechanism: Pioneer species (fast-growth, sun-loving, short-lived) establish first. Canopy closes (stem-exclusion). Small disturbances open canopy GAPS in which SHADE-TOLERANT species recruit and slowly grow (understory-reinitiation). The system progresses to OLD-GROWTH (climax) where only species capable of regenerating in their own shade persist. Self-shade-tolerance is the climax selection criterion.
llm_application: Four-stage forest-succession training schedule: (1) PIONEER pretrain on broad fast-coverage corpus to establish all basic capabilities. (2) STEM-EXCLUSION: introduce competitive losses across capability heads forcing specialization. (3) UNDERSTORY-REINITIATION (NOVEL CORE): periodic GAP DYNAMICS — remove a small fraction of highest-magnitude weights and admit slow-LR retraining inside the gap, recruiting shade-tolerant (low-magnitude-stable) capabilities that couldn't establish under high-magnitude competition. (4) OLD-GROWTH: prune any weight that cannot generate self-consistent fine-tunes — climax self-shade-tolerance selection. The gap-dynamics + self-shade-tolerance climax selection are the distinguishing mechanisms.
novelty_claim: Self-Evolving Curriculum (2505.14970), Knowledge-Circuits Phase Shift (2502.11196), Self-Evolving LLM via Intrinsic Feedback (2510.02752) approach phase-aware training but do not implement the 4-stage forest-succession discipline with explicit gap-dynamics weight removal + climax self-shade-tolerance selection. The shade-tolerance / gap-recruit / climax-self-shade training schedule is not retrieved in surveyed prior art.
